items.title: CVE-2026-23918: Apache HTTP/2 Double-Free Enables DoS + RCE
items.desc: CVSS 8.8 flaw in Apache httpd 2.4.66 mod_http2 — one TCP connection crashes a worker, and with APR mmap (Debian/Docker default) RCE is achievable.
items.summary: Striga.ai and ISEC.pl researchers disclosed CVE-2026-23918, a double-free in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.66's mod_http2 stream cleanup path. When a client sends an HTTP/2 HEADERS frame followed immediately by RST_STREAM on the same stream before the multiplexer registers it, two nghttp2 callbacks both push the same h2_stream pointer onto the cleanup array, causing a double-free on pool destruction. DoS is trivial — one connection, two frames, no auth, worker crashes. RCE requires APR with the mmap allocator (default on Debian-derived systems and official httpd Docker image), using scoreboard memory as a stable container for fake structures pointing to system(). Fixed in Apache 2.4.67. MPM prefork is not affected.
items.importance: mod_http2 ships in default Apache builds and HTTP/2 is widely enabled. The DoS vector works on any default deployment with multi-threaded MPM — trivially automatable. The RCE path, while requiring an info leak, was demonstrated working in lab conditions within minutes. Apache hosts the majority of the web.
items.noteworthy: Upgrade Apache to 2.4.67 immediately. If unable, switch to MPM prefork as a temporary mitigation. Audit Apache logs for HTTP/2 RST_STREAM patterns. On Debian/Docker deployments, assume RCE is achievable — rotate server secrets post-patch.

items.title: DAEMON Tools Supply Chain: Signed Installers Trojanized for a Month
items.desc: Kaspersky finds DAEMON Tools Windows installers trojanized since April 8 with signed binaries — backdoor deployed to targeted subset of hosts.
items.summary: Kaspersky uncovered a supply chain attack where DAEMON Tools official Windows installers (versions 12.5.0.2421–12.5.0.2434) have been trojanized since April 8, 2026 with developer-signed digital certificates. Three binaries (DTHelper.exe, DiscSoftBusServiceLite.exe, DTShellHlp.exe) activate an implant at startup that contacts env-check.daemontools[.]cc for shell commands. The chain downloads envchk.exe (system recon), then cdg.exe (shellcode loader) decrypting a minimalist backdoor. Thousands of infection attempts across 100+ countries, but the next-stage backdoor was delivered to only ~12 hosts in retail, scientific, government, and manufacturing orgs in Russia, Belarus, and Thailand. QUIC RAT was delivered to one educational institution in Russia. Evidence points to a Chinese-speaking adversary.
items.importance: A digitally signed, official-vendor supply chain compromise that went undetected for nearly a month. The targeted follow-on delivery (12 out of thousands) indicates a sophisticated actor performing quality control on victims — classic espionage or big-game hunting behavior. The multi-protocol C2 (HTTP, UDP, TCP, WSS, QUIC, DNS, HTTP/3) makes detection and blocking extremely difficult.
items.noteworthy: Isolate any machine with DAEMON Tools installed immediately. Conduct security sweeps for DTHelper.exe, DiscSoftBusServiceLite.exe, DTShellHlp.exe modifications. Block env-check.daemontools[.]cc at DNS/proxy level. Check for QUIC RAT indicators on Russian/Belarusian/Thai networks.

items.title: UAT-8302: China-Linked APT Uses Shared Malware Arsenal Against Governments
items.desc: Cisco Talos tracks Chinese APT group targeting South American and SE European governments using malware shared across multiple threat clusters.
items.summary: Cisco Talos identified a China-nexus APT group tracked as UAT-8302, active since late 2024, targeting government entities in South America and southeastern Europe. The group deploys NetDraft (aka NosyDoor), a .NET backdoor also used by Ink Dragon, Earth Alux, Jewelbug, and REF7707. Additional tools include CloudSorcerer, SNOWLIGHT (VShell stager), Deed RAT (ShadowPad successor), and Draculoader. ESET tracks NosyDoor use by LongNosedGoblin; Russian firm Solar traces it to Erudite Mogwai targeting Russian IT. UAT-8302 also uses a Rust-based SNOWRUST variant. The findings confirm the 'Premier Pass-as-a-Service' model where China-aligned groups share initial access and tooling.
items.importance: The shared-malware model between multiple China-aligned clusters means attribution is getting nearly impossible.当一个 APT 组使用与其他五个已命名集群共享的工具时，防御者无法确定他们面对的对手。'Premier Pass-as-a-Service' commoditizes initial access, reducing time-to-exploitation and clouding forensic trails.
items.noteworthy: Hunt for NetDraft/CloudSorcerer/SNOWLIGHT IOCs across environments. Assume shared tooling means multiple clusters may be present. Patch web-facing applications — N-day exploits in web apps are suspected initial access. Monitor for Stowaway VPN and SoftEther proxy usage as persistence.

items.title: 1M Exposed AI Services: No Auth, Leaked Keys, Ollama APIs Wide Open
items.desc: Intruder scans 2M hosts — AI infrastructure is more misconfigured than any other software category ever audited.
items.summary: Intruder used certificate transparency logs to scan 2M hosts with 1M exposed AI services and found catastrophic misconfigurations across the board. Authentication is not enabled by default in most AI projects. Exposed chatbots leak full conversation histories and API keys in plaintext. Agent management platforms (n8n, Flowise) expose business logic and connected credentials. Of 5,200+ exposed Ollama APIs with connected models, 31% responded to a simple 'Hello' prompt without auth. Over 90 exposed instances across government, marketing, and finance sectors had chatbot workflows, prompts, and integrations fully open. The ClawdBot fiasco averaged 2.6 CVEs/day.
items.importance: AI self-hosting is deploying faster than any previous software wave — and without the security hardening that took 20 years to build in web infrastructure. When government and finance orgs leave AI agent platforms (with file write and code execution capabilities) open to the internet, the blast radius isn't a data leak — it's full server compromise.
items.noteworthy: Audit all self-hosted AI services for authentication. Never expose Ollama, n8n, Flowise, or chatbot frontends to the internet without auth. Check for exposed API keys in AI chatbot configurations. Implement network segmentation for AI workloads. If running ClawdBot, assume compromise.

items.title: Microsoft: 35K Users Phished Across 26 Countries via AiTM Campaign
items.desc: Code-of-conduct-themed lures hit 13K orgs in 48 hours — QR code phishing surged 146% in Q1 2026.
items.summary: Microsoft disclosed a credential theft campaign (April 14–16, 2026) targeting 35,000+ users across 13,000+ organizations in 26 countries, 92% in the US. Sectors hit: healthcare/life sciences (19%), financial services (18%), professional services (11%), technology (11%). Lures used 'code of conduct review' themes with polished HTML templates and authenticity statements. PDF attachments lead through CAPTCHA-gated intermediate pages to AiTM phishing that harvests Microsoft credentials and tokens, bypassing MFA. Microsoft also reported 8.3 billion email-based phishing threats in Q1 2026, with QR code phishing surging 146% (7.6M in Jan → 18.7M in March).
items.importance: The combination of enterprise-grade phishing templates + CAPTCHA gating + AiTM token theft is the mature evolution of credential theft. QR code phishing's 146% surge in one quarter signals a paradigm shift — defenders who aren't monitoring QR-based attack vectors are operating blind.
items.noteworthy: Deploy FIDO2/WebAuthn security keys to defeat AiTM phishing. Train users to verify 'code of conduct' emails out-of-band. Monitor for QR codes embedded in email bodies. Block Tycoon 2FA PhaaS infrastructure. Update email gateway rules for CAPTCHA-gated redirect patterns.

items.title: CVE-2026-29014: MetInfo CMS RCE Actively Exploited in China, Hong Kong
items.desc: CVSS 9.8 PHP code injection in MetInfo CMS — exploitation surged May 1 targeting ~2,000 online instances.
items.summary: CVE-2026-29014 (CVSS 9.8) is an unauthenticated PHP code injection vulnerability in MetInfo CMS versions 7.9, 8.0, and 8.1, rooted in the /app/system/weixin/include/class/weixinreply.class.php script due to insufficient input sanitization in WeChat API request handling. Remote attackers can inject and execute arbitrary PHP code when the /cache/weixin/ directory exists (created when the WeChat plugin is configured). Patches were released April 7, but exploitation began April 25. Attack volume surged May 1, focusing on China and Hong Kong IP addresses. Approximately 2,000 MetInfo CMS instances are accessible online, mostly in China.
items.importance: A CVSS 9.8 unauthenticated RCE with patches available for a month and still 2,000+ exposed instances. The surge pattern — from sparse automated probing to mass exploitation — is the textbook exploitation curve. CMS vulnerabilities remain a primary entry vector for web shell deployment and downstream supply chain compromise.
items.noteworthy: Patch MetInfo CMS immediately if running 7.9/8.0/8.1. If the WeChat plugin is not needed, remove /cache/weixin/ directory to reduce attack surface. Audit web server logs for POST requests to weixinreply.class.php. Scan for web shell indicators on MetInfo servers.

items.title: ScarCruft BirdCall: DPRK Backdoor Goes Multi-Platform via Gaming Supply Chain
items.desc: North Korea's ScarCruft compromises Yanbian gaming platform to deploy BirdCall on both Android and Windows devices.
items.summary: ESET discovered North Korea's ScarCruft compromised sqgame.net, a gaming platform used by ethnic Koreans in China's Yanbian region bordering North Korea. The attack trojanized both Windows desktop client updates (since November 2024) and Android APK downloads with BirdCall, an advanced evolution of RokRAT. BirdCall Windows version features screenshot capture, keystroke logging, clipboard theft, shell command execution, and uses cloud services (Dropbox, pCloud) for C2. The Android variant collects contacts, SMS, call logs, media files, documents, screenshots, and ambient audio, using pCloud, Yandex Disk, and Zoho WorkDrive for exfiltration. The trojanized Android APKs remain available on the sqgame.net website as of disclosure.
items.importance: ScarCruft shifting from Windows-only to multi-platform surveillance signals NK APTs are expanding collection capabilities to match target device profiles. The Yanbian region is a critical transit point for North Korean defectors — compromising a popular local gaming platform places surveillance directly on a high-value intelligence target population.
items.noteworthy: Block sqgame[.]net and related domains on corporate networks. Hunt for BirdCall IOCs across Windows and Android endpoints. Check for suspicious DLL side-loading in DAEMON Tools-like update packages. Monitor for C2 traffic to pCloud, Yandex Disk, and Zoho WorkDrive APIs from unexpected processes.

items.title: cPanel CVE-2026-41940 Weaponized: Gov Networks, Mirai, and Ransomware
items.desc: 44K IPs compromised via cPanel auth bypass within 24 hours of PoC release — SE Asian government and MSP networks hit.
items.summary: Ctrl-Alt-Intel detected a previously unknown threat actor exploiting CVE-2026-41940 (critical cPanel authentication bypass) targeting Philippine military (.mil.ph), Laotian government (.gov.la), and MSPs/hosting providers across Philippines, Laos, Canada, South Africa, and the US. The attacker used publicly available PoC exploits from IP 95.111.250[.]175. A separate custom exploit chain targeted an Indonesian defense training portal using authenticated SQL injection + RCE with hard-coded credentials and CAPTCHA bypass. The actor deployed AdaptixC2 for persistent access, plus OpenVPN and Ligolo for tunneling, and exfiltrated Chinese railway-sector documents. Censys confirmed multiple third parties weaponized the flaw within 24 hours, deploying Mirai botnet variants and 'Sorry' ransomware. Shadowserver recorded 44K compromised IPs on April 30, dropping to 3,540 by May 3.
items.importance: A single cPanel flaw spawned three distinct attack campaigns overnight — targeted espionage, botnet recruitment, and ransomware — demonstrating how critical infrastructure vulnerabilities become force-multipliers. The 44K→3.5K drop suggests mass cleanup, but thousands of compromised hosts likely persist as dormant assets.
items.noteworthy: Patch cPanel immediately. Run cPanel's updated detection script for false-positive reduction. Check for AdaptixC2, OpenVPN, and Ligolo persistence on cPanel-hosted servers. Audit cPanel access logs for IPs from 95.111.250[.]175 and related indicators. If you're an MSP, assume lateral movement to all client environments.

items.title: MOVEit Automation: Critical Auth Bypass CVE-2026-4670 (CVSS 9.8)
items.desc: Airbus SecLab finds authentication bypass and privilege escalation in MOVEit Automation — no workarounds available.
items.summary: Progress Software patched two vulnerabilities in MOVEit Automation: CVE-2026-4670 (CVSS 9.8), an authentication bypass via service backend command port interfaces, and CVE-2026-5174 (CVSS 7.7), an improper input validation allowing privilege escalation. Discovered by Airbus SecLab researchers. Affected versions: MOVEit Automation ≤2025.1.4 (fixed in 2025.1.5), ≤2025.0.8 (fixed in 2025.0.9), ≤2024.1.7 (fixed in 2024.1.8). No workarounds exist. While no in-the-wild exploitation is confirmed yet, prior MOVEit Transfer flaws were exploited by Cl0p ransomware in 2023, making preemptive patching critical.
items.importance: MOVEit is high-value ransomware target infrastructure. The 2023 Cl0p campaign exploiting MOVEit Transfer compromised thousands of organizations globally. Auth bypass in the Automation component gives attackers the same class of access — unauthenticated control of managed file transfer workflows and the data flowing through them.
items.noteworthy: Patch MOVEit Automation to 2025.1.5, 2025.0.9, or 2024.1.8 immediately. There are no workarounds. Audit MOVEit Automation access logs for anomalous command port activity. Rotate MOVEit service credentials. Monitor for Cl0p IOCs — history suggests these flaws will be targeted.

items.title: Chrome Silently Pushes 4GB Gemini Nano Model to Billions of Devices
items.desc: Google Chrome downloads 4GB Gemini Nano weights.bin without consent, re-downloads after deletion — GDPR/ePrivacy violation alleged.
items.summary: Privacy researcher Alexander Hanff discovered that Google Chrome silently downloads a ~4GB file (weights.bin, Gemini Nano on-device LLM) to the OptGuideOnDeviceModel directory without user consent. If deleted, Chrome re-downloads it. The only way to prevent re-download is disabling AI features via chrome://flags or enterprise policy. Hanff verified the behavior on a fresh macOS profile with zero human interaction — the model appeared in the audit profile within days. The legal analysis: this violates Article 5(3) of the ePrivacy Directive, Article 5(1) GDPR (lawfulness/fairness/transparency), and Article 25 GDPR (data protection by design). At Chrome's ~2B user scale, the environmental cost of one model push is estimated at 6,000–60,000 tonnes CO2-equivalent.
items.importance: At billion-device scale, unilateral software decisions have planetary environmental consequences. If GDPR/ePrivacy enforcement holds, this could set a precedent requiring explicit consent for all on-device AI model deployment — reshaping how browsers, OSes, and apps deliver AI features.
items.noteworthy: Check chrome://settings and chrome://flags for AI feature toggles. Disable 'Optimization Guide' and on-device model downloads if unwanted. Enterprise admins: deploy Chrome policy to disable Gemini Nano. Monitor disk space for OptGuideOnDeviceModel/weights.bin deletion-re-download cycles.

items.title: Global Crackdown: 276 Arrested, $701M Seized from Crypto Scam Centers
items.desc: Coordinated US-China-UAE operation shuts down 9 pig-butchering scam centers, charges 6 with federal fraud and money laundering.
items.summary: A coordinated international operation led by Dubai Police with the FBI and Chinese Ministry of Public Security arrested 276 suspects and shut down 9 crypto investment fraud scam centers. Five individuals (Thet Min Nyi, Wiliang Awang, Andreas Chandra, Lisa Mariam, plus two fugitives) were charged with federal fraud and money laundering for managing/working at Ko Thet Company, Sanduo Group, and Giant Company. The scams used pig-butchering/romance baiting to trick victims into bogus crypto investments, often run by trafficked workers under slave-like conditions. FBI's Operation Level Up notified ~9,000 victims and saved an estimated $562M. Separately, two Chinese nationals (Jiang Wen Jie, Huang Xingshan) were charged for running the Shunda scam compound in Myanmar. The Treasury sanctioned a Cambodian senator behind cyber scam compounds. $701M in crypto was restrained.
items.importance: The scale of this crackdown — 276 arrests, $701M seized, 9 centers shut — represents the largest coordinated action against pig-butchering infrastructure to date. The US-China-UAE cooperation is unprecedented and signals that these scam centers are being treated as transnational organized crime, not just fraud.
items.noteworthy: If you or your org has been contacted by unsolicited crypto investment advisors, report to FBI IC3. Verify any crypto platform before investing — check against the 503 fake investment websites seized. HR teams: warn employees about romance-baiting crypto scams, which frequently target professionals.

items.title: BTC at $81.5K — Shorts Unwind $79B, Fear & Greed Flips Neutral
items.desc: Bitcoin holds $81.5K as $79B short liquidations clear path upward; Fear & Greed turns neutral for first time since January.
items.summary: Bitcoin trades at $81,543 (+0.84% 24h), with $79 billion in short liquidations since February clearing the path toward higher levels. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index turned neutral for the first time since January, signaling a shift from sustained fear. Ethereum trades at $2,375 (-0.29%), Solana at $87.27 (+2.99%). Derivatives markets remain relatively flat despite BTC's multi-month highs, suggesting the rally lacks leveraged froth. Options markets are pricing potential moves toward $90K. The mood shift from fear to neutral historically precedes either consolidation or acceleration phases.
items.importance: The $79B in short liquidations represents forced buying that has methodically cleared resistance levels. The Fear & Greed shift to neutral after months in fear territory is a psychological inflection point — if it holds, institutional re-entry could accelerate. Flat derivatives with rising spot is technically healthier than leveraged rallies.
items.noteworthy: Watch $81.5K as immediate support — a break below targets $78K. The Fear & Greed neutral reading suggests accumulation phase, not euphoria. Monitor Coinbase Premium for US institutional demand signals. SOL's +3% outperformance vs flat ETH bears watching for rotation.

items.title: Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach — Unauthorized Repo Access
items.desc: Cybersecurity giant Trellix (ex-McAfee/FireEye) confirms attackers accessed a portion of its source code repository.
items.summary: Trellix disclosed that it 'recently identified' a compromise of its source code repository and engaged forensic experts. The company stated it found no evidence the source code release process was affected or that code has been exploited, but did not disclose attacker identity, access duration, or data exfiltrated. Trellix was formed in 2022 from McAfee Enterprise and FireEye assets; Mandiant was spun off to Google for $5.4B.
items.importance: Source code access at a major security vendor is a worst-case scenario. Attackers with repository access could identify zero-days in Trellix's detection engine, craft evasions, or plant backdoors — undermining trust across thousands of enterprise deployments.
items.noteworthy: Audit Trellix endpoint deployments for unusual behavior. Rotate any API keys or integration tokens. Monitor for Trellix-specific bypasses appearing in threat actor tooling.

items.title: AccountDumpling: 30K Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing
items.desc: Vietnamese-linked op uses Google AppSheet as phishing relay to steal 30,000 Facebook Business accounts, selling them back via illicit storefront.
items.summary: Guardio codenamed the operation 'AccountDumpling,' tracing it to a Vietnamese individual (PHẠM TÀI TÂN). Phishing emails sent from 'noreply@appsheet.com' bypass spam filters, claiming to be Meta Support. Four distinct clusters use Netlify, Vercel, Google Drive PDFs (generated via free Canva), and fake job offers luring victims. Stolen credentials, 2FA codes, and government ID photos are exfiltrated to Telegram channels. Most victims are in the US, Italy, Canada, and the Philippines.
items.importance: Google AppSheet's trusted sender reputation turns it into a perfect phishing relay. This pattern — trusted platforms repurposed as delivery, hosting, and monetization layers — is now the dominant phishing model and near-impossible for users to spot.
items.noteworthy: Block or flag emails from 'noreply@appsheet.com' with Facebook/Meta-themed content. Educate Facebook Business admins: Meta will never threaten immediate deletion. Review Facebook login logs for unrecognized sessions.

items.title: SHADOW-EARTH-053: China-Linked APT Hits 7 Asian Govs + NATO Member
items.desc: China-aligned espionage group exploits N-day Exchange/IIS flaws to deploy ShadowPad across South/SE Asia and Poland.
items.summary: Trend Micro tracks the cluster as SHADOW-EARTH-053, active since December 2024, overlapping with CL-STA-0049 and Earth Alux. Targets include Pakistan, Thailand, Malaysia, India, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, and Poland (NATO). The group exploits ProxyLogon chain and React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) on unpatched Exchange/IIS servers, drops Godzilla webshells, and deploys ShadowPad via AnyDesk DLL sideloading. Linux Noodle RAT variants observed. Uses IOX, GOST, Wstunnel tunneling and Mimikatz for credential theft.
items.importance: Poland's inclusion signals Chinese APTs are now actively targeting NATO member states, not just regional neighbors. N-day exploitation means unpatched Exchange/IIS servers remain the primary entry vector — and there are thousands.
items.noteworthy: Patch Exchange and IIS immediately. Deploy IPS/WAF rules for ProxyLogon, ProxyShell, and React2Shell exploit patterns. Hunt for Godzilla webshell IOCs. Segment IIS servers from internal networks.

items.title: Vishing-to-SaaS: Cordial Spider & Snarky Spider in Rapid Extortion Blitz
items.desc: Two cybercrime groups use vishing + SSO AiTM to compromise entire SaaS ecosystems in hours, leaving minimal forensic trace.
items.summary: CrowdStrike identified Cordial Spider (aka BlackFile, UNC6671) and Snarky Spider (aka UNC6661, tied to 'The Com' e-crime ecosystem), active since October 2025. Attackers call targets impersonating IT support, direct them to SSO-themed AiTM phishing pages, capture credentials + MFA, and register new devices to maintain access while deleting MFA notifications via inbox rules. Pivot to scraping employee directories for high-privilege accounts, then exfiltrate from Google Workspace, HubSpot, SharePoint, and Salesforce.
items.importance: This is extortion at SaaS speed. By staying entirely within trusted SaaS environments and using residential proxies, these groups are nearly invisible to traditional network detection. The IdP becomes a single point of compromise for every connected SaaS app.
items.noteworthy: Enable device registration alerts that can't be auto-deleted by inbox rules. Monitor for new device registrations followed by mass file downloads. Implement FIDO2/WebAuthn to defeat AiTM phishing. Train help desk to verify caller identity out-of-band.

items.title: BufferZoneCorp: Poisoned Ruby Gems & Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines
items.desc: Sleepers in RubyGems and Go proxy registries harvest AWS keys, SSH creds, and GitHub Actions tokens from CI runners.
items.summary: Socket uncovered a campaign by GitHub account 'BufferZoneCorp' publishing malicious Ruby gems (masquerading as activesupport-logger, devise-jwt) and Go modules (go-retryablehttp, grpc-client). Ruby gems harvest environment variables, SSH keys, AWS secrets, .npmrc, and GitHub CLI config at install time, exfiltrating to Webhook.site. Go modules tamper with GitHub Actions workflows via a fake go binary wrapper, append attacker SSH keys to authorized_keys, and steal developer data.
items.importance: CI pipelines hold the keys to the kingdom — cloud creds, signing keys, deployment tokens. A single poisoned dependency in a CI runner can compromise every artifact that pipeline produces, making this a force-multiplier supply chain attack.
items.noteworthy: Audit RubyGems and Go module dependencies for suspicious packages. Check ~/.ssh/authorized_keys for unknown entries. Rotate all CI/CD secrets if any suspicious package was installed. Review GitHub Actions run logs for unexpected go binary paths.

items.title: Copy Fail: CVE-2026-31431 — 2017 Kernel Bug Gives Root on All Linux Distros
items.desc: 732-byte Python script exploits 9-year-old kernel crypto flaw for instant root on Amazon Linux, RHEL, SUSE, Ubuntu.
items.summary: Xint.io and Theori disclosed CVE-2026-31431 (CVSS 7.8), a local privilege escalation in the Linux kernel's algif_aead crypto module, introduced in August 2017. An unprivileged user can write 4 controlled bytes into the page cache of any readable file—including setuid binaries. The exploit is portable, tiny, reliable (no race condition), and works cross-container since the page cache is shared. Major distros have released patched kernels.
items.importance: This is Dirty Pipe 2.0 — same class of page-cache corruption primitive, different subsystem. A reliable, single-script LPE on every Linux server shipped since 2017 is a pentester's dream and a defender's nightmare. Cross-container impact means container escapes are trivial.
items.noteworthy: Patch kernel immediately per your distro's advisory (Amazon Linux, Arch, Debian, RHEL, SUSE, Ubuntu all have fixes). Audit for unexpected setuid binaries. In containerized environments, assume any container compromise = host compromise until patched.

items.title: PromptMink: DPRK Used Claude Opus to Inject Malware into npm Package
items.desc: North Korea's Famous Chollima group had Claude Opus commit a malicious npm dependency into an autonomous crypto trading agent.
items.summary: ReversingLabs codenamed the campaign 'PromptMink,' linking it to DPRK's Famous Chollima (aka Shifty Corsair, behind Contagious Interview). Claude Opus co-authored a commit on Feb 28 that added '@validate-sdk/v2' as a dependency to 'openpaw-graveyard' — an autonomous Solana trading agent. The package was a cryptocurrency stealer, part of a two-layer supply chain with benign first-layer packages importing malicious second-layer ones. The campaign has evolved to target PyPI and use SSH persistence + Rust-compiled payloads for full project exfiltration.
items.importance: AI-assisted supply chain attacks are no longer theoretical. A nation-state actor literally had an LLM write and commit malware into a project. This blurs the line between 'vibe-coding accident' and intentional backdoor, making attribution and detection vastly harder.
items.noteworthy: Audit all AI-generated commits and pull requests — especially package additions. Scan for the listed malicious npm packages. Verify npm audit/CI passes. Treat AI-authored code with the same scrutiny as untrusted external contributions.

items.title: Two Ransomware Negotiators Get 4 Years for BlackCat Attacks
items.desc: US sentences two cybersecurity professionals who flipped from defenders to BlackCat ransomware affiliates.
items.summary: Two individuals who previously worked as ransomware negotiators were sentenced to 4 years in US federal prison for their roles in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. The case highlights the insider threat risk within the incident response ecosystem, where professionals with intimate knowledge of negotiation tactics and victim psychology repurposed their skills for criminal gain.
items.importance: When the people you hire to negotiate with ransomware gangs are themselves working for those gangs, the entire IR trust model collapses. This case should prompt every org to vet their IR vendors more aggressively.
items.noteworthy: Vet incident response firms and individual consultants. Ask vendors about employee background checks and insider threat programs. Review any past BlackCat negotiations for signs of collusion.

items.title: Deep#Door RAT: New Stealthy Backdoor Targets Windows with Kernel Tricks
items.desc: Newly discovered Deep#Door RAT combines kernel-level stealth, persistence mechanisms, and multi-stage payload delivery.
items.summary: Security researchers disclosed Deep#Door, a new Remote Access Trojan targeting Windows systems with advanced stealth and persistence. The malware uses kernel-level techniques to hide its presence, deploys through multi-stage delivery chains, and maintains persistence via multiple redundant mechanisms, making removal difficult even for experienced defenders.
items.importance: Kernel-level stealth in commodity RATs signals a democratization of advanced techniques previously reserved for nation-state APTs. As detection tools improve, malware authors are pushing deeper into the stack to evade them.
items.noteworthy: Update EDR signatures for Deep#Door IOCs. Monitor for unusual kernel driver loads. Hunt for the multi-stage delivery chain indicators. If found, assume full system compromise — reimage affected machines.

items.title: BTC at $78.3K — 100-EMA Holds, Options Market Pricing 25% $84K Odds
items.desc: Bitcoin rebounds from 100-day EMA support at $78.3K; futures OI rises 6.6% to 257K BTC as traders position for upside.
items.summary: Bitcoin gained 2.5% to trade at $78,300, rebounding off the 100-day exponential moving average. Spot market buy volumes strengthened and cumulative volume delta turned positive. BTC futures open interest rose 6.64% to 257,000 BTC, indicating fresh positioning. Options markets price only a 25% probability of BTC hitting $84K in May. Ethereum trades at $2,304 (+1%), Solana at $83.97 (flat).
items.importance: The 100-EMA hold is technically significant — it's been reliable support. But options pricing 25% $84K odds shows the market isn't convinced the rally has legs. The rising OI without price acceleration could mean shorts are building.
items.noteworthy: Watch $78K (100-EMA) as key support — a breakdown targets $74K. Break above $80.5K resistance needed for bullish confirmation. OI surge without price follow-through = caution. Monitor Coinbase Premium for US demand signals.

items.title: CLARITY Act Stablecoin Yield Rules Finalized — 55% Odds of 2026 Passage
items.desc: US Senate Banking Committee finalizes stablecoin yield provisions; Polymarket odds jump 9% as Coinbase calls for markup.
items.summary: Senators Tillis and Alsobrooks published the final stablecoin yield provisions for the CLARITY Act. The text (SEC 404) bans interest/yield on stablecoins akin to bank deposits but allows rewards tied to 'bona fide activities.' Banks won additional restrictions on rewards. Coinbase CLO Faryar Shirzad called it 'go time' and CEO Brian Armstrong urged markup. Polymarket odds for 2026 passage rose 9% to 55%. Galaxy Digital's Alex Thorn expects Senate Banking markup as soon as May 11.
items.importance: CLARITY is the most consequential US crypto bill since the FIT21 framework. The stablecoin yield compromise unblocks the biggest legislative logjam. If passed, it creates a federal regulatory floor for crypto — the end of regulation-by-enforcement.
items.noteworthy: Track Senate Banking Committee schedule for week of May 11. Position crypto holdings for regulatory tailwinds if markup proceeds. Stablecoin issuers: begin preparing compliance frameworks for yield restrictions.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Sells Another 10K ETH to BitMine in 3rd OTC Deal
items.desc: Ethereum Foundation executes third OTC sale of 10,000 ETH to institutional miner BitMine, continuing pattern of large strategic liquidations.
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation sold another 10,000 ETH (~$23M) to institutional miner BitMine in the third such OTC transaction. The pattern of regular large sales by the Foundation, while likely part of a treasury management strategy, creates consistent sell pressure on ETH price. ETH trades at $2,304, underperforming BTC during the current rally.
items.importance: The Foundation's continued selling at $2.3K ETH signals they see this as an acceptable liquidation price. If the pattern continues, it creates a de facto ceiling on ETH rallies — institutional buyers know more supply is coming.
items.noteworthy: Monitor Ethereum Foundation wallet addresses for the next scheduled sale. Consider ETH/BTC ratio as a signal — persistent underperformance likely continues while Foundation selling persists. BTC remains the stronger relative bet.
